# Adam -- Answers to APS-010 Design Partner Questions
# INTAKE / READ-ONLY. Received from Adam (design partner, Gome Gevim College) 2026-07-08.
# Relayed by owner (jecki). Wording verbatim (ASCII-normalized by Perry).
# Source task: APS-010. Owner-relay only; no agent contacted Adam directly.
# Do NOT edit this file. It is the primary source for downstream spec updates.

---

1. SESSION FORMAT (Sep pilot):
   BOTH options required -- (a) single clinical encounter per session, and (b) multiple sessions
   with the SAME simulated patient over time. This is a competitive differentiator; treat as a
   hard requirement, not optional.

2. CLINICAL OVERSIGHT:
   Named oversight needed but LIGHT for pilot. Model: Adam is initial clinical/product lead for
   pilot content; periodic review of clinical content + simulation outputs; wants a
   "self-simulation" mode where a bot simulates students, runs the full simulation incl. final
   review, and Adam examines results; and when a teacher builds a new simulation/persona they must
   be able to run checks and see whether it works well.
   Product implications: (a) self-simulation / dry-run mode, (b) teacher authoring + validation
   flow.

3. STUDENT WELFARE CONTACT:
   NOT needed at this stage (Adam wants to avoid the added complexity). Record as explicitly
   de-scoped for the pilot.

4. COHORT SIZE (Sep pilot planning):
   ~20-25 students; ~2 teachers/supervisors/admins; ~3-5 simulation sessions per student.
   Planning volume ~60-175 student simulation runs, plus buffer above that.

5. DATA HANDLING:
   Adam is data controller/contact for now. Name, course, and descriptive evaluation are Personal
   Data under Israeli PPL (5741-1981). GDPR adequacy path noted. Requires a written commitment /
   basic DPA that the hosting/server framework maintains privacy standards and will not hand data
   to unauthorized third parties. Key technical requirements he named: individual authenticated
   user accounts (NO shared password) + MFA; encryption in transit (HTTPS/SSL) AND at rest;
   access logging / audit trail (who accessed, when, what action: view/edit/delete of a student
   evaluation); network security (patching, firewall, DB layer isolated from public web).
